title: 10 best herbal capsules for dehydration

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with dehydration because they contain antioxidants that help to replenish and balance the body's electrolyte levels. The herbs in these capsules, such as elderberry and burdock root, have natural diuretic properties that stimulate urine production, encouraging the body to flush out excess fluids and toxins. Additionally, elder capsules also provide essential vitamins and minerals like potassium, calcium, and magnesium, which are vital for maintaining proper hydration and supporting overall bodily functions.
remedy_properties: Vasoprotective and antioxidant properties: Elderberry's rich anthocyanin content helps protect the blood vessels from damage, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to dehydration.

Immune-modulating effects: Elderberry's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to stimulate the immune system, helping to boost the body's natural defense against infections that can lead to dehydration.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elderberry's polyphenols have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of dehydration such as diarrhea and vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and improve the body's ability to absorb and retain fluids, making them beneficial in combating dehydration.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been shown to have diuretic properties, helping the body to remove excess fluids and toxins, while also supporting the kidneys in their function to prevent dehydration.

Ellagic acid: This polyphenol has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to protect the kidneys from damage caused by dehydration, thereby supporting the body's natural ability to maintain fluid balance.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Sambucus nigra for making capsules, as they contain high levels of bioactive compounds and are believed to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems may be used, as they contain some bioactive compounds, but this is less common due to lower concentrations.
Fruits: The fruits are sometimes used, as they contain some bioactive compounds, but their use is less common due to lower concentrations compared to the flowers.
remedy_recipe: Collect 500 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Grind the collected material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered material with 80 grams of carrier material such as microcrystalline cellulose.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and produce 200 capsules using a 1 gram weight setting.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dry place.

remedy_recipe: Source high-quality dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable supplier that are free of contaminants and heavy metals.

Use a capsule filling machine to fill 5000 empty vegetable capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture at a dosage of 120mg per capsule.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves in the early morning, avoiding any wilted or damaged plants.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 2-3 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried lavandula angustifolia leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herb with gelatin capsules in a ratio of 1 part herb to 10 parts capsule material, filling each capsule by hand.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months, ready for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram capsules helps with dehydration because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help regulate fluid balance in the body. The herbs' diuretic properties also aid in increasing urine production, which can help eliminate excess fluids and toxins from the body. Additionally, wild marjoram's antispasmodic effects help to relax muscles and reduce muscle cramps caused by dehydration, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall hydration and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Wild marjoram's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to dehydration, protecting the body from oxidative stress and promoting overall hydration.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in wild marjoram capsules reduce inflammation in the body, which can exacerbate dehydration symptoms such as headaches and fatigue, allowing for better rehydration.

Diuretic Properties: Wild marjoram's natural diuretic properties help increase urine production, promoting the removal of excess fluids and electrolytes from the body, while also reducing fluid retention that can contribute to dehydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wild marjoram contain high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to rehydrate the body and replenish lost electrolytes.
Fruits: Wild marjoram fruits have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing water retention and relieving symptoms of dehydration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves from a trusted source to ensure potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with gelatin capsules in a 1:1 ratio by weight, filling 100 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with dehydration because they contain compounds that help regulate fluid balance in the body. The essential oils present in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, have natural diuretic properties, which aid in increasing urine production to flush out excess toxins and electrolytes from the body. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help protect cells from damage caused by dehydration, promoting healthy hydration levels and reducing oxidative stress. By supplementing with rosemary capsules, individuals can effectively alleviate mild to moderate dehydration symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Rosemary's antioxidants help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dehydration, allowing the body to better conserve water and maintain hydration levels.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to dehydration, and also alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue associated with dehydration.

Improved blood circulation: Rosemary's vasodilatory properties can help improve blood circulation, allowing for more efficient transport of oxygen and nutrients to the body's cells, including those that regulate water balance and hydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary have been traditionally used to combat dehydration due to their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Buds: Rosemary buds are rich in essential oils, particularly camphor, which can help to reduce fluid loss and alleviate symptoms of dehydration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of rosmarinus officinalis in quantities sufficient for the desired batch size.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or oven at low temperature for 30 minutes to preserve potency.
Grind dried leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or food processor for efficient extraction.
Mix the ground leaf powder with gelatin or plant-derived binder in a ratio of 1:2 to create a uniform blend.
Fill clean capsules with the herbal blend at a dosage of 500mg per capsule and store in airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date capsules helps with dehydration because they are rich in natural compounds that promote fluid balance and retention in the body. The dates used to make these capsules are high in potassium, an essential electrolyte that helps regulate water levels in the body. Additionally, the antioxidants and flavonoids present in the dates have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and protect the kidneys, allowing them to function optimally and eliminate excess fluids efficiently. This natural combination of nutrients and compounds helps to alleviate dehydration symptoms and promote overall hydration.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: The dates used in these capsules contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to dehydration.

Replenishing electrolytes and minerals: Dates are rich in potassium, sodium, and other essential minerals that help replenish fluids and electrolytes in the body, reducing dehydration symptoms such as fatigue, weakness, and cramping.

Stabilizing blood sugar levels: The natural sugars present in dates help regulate blood sugar levels, which can become imbalanced during dehydration. This helps maintain proper bodily functions and reduces the risk of further dehydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat dehydration, as they help to restore the body's natural balance and promote hydration.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Chinese Date plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that can help to prevent dehydration by promoting water absorption in the body.
remedy_recipe: Collect 100 grams of dried ziziphus jujuba fruit and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground powder with 200 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and simmer for 30 minutes over low heat.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove any solids and discard the residue.
Add 100 grams of gelatin and mix until it is fully dissolved, then fill clean gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the herbal extract.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer storage.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for dehydration are natural supplements that help replenish fluids and electrolytes in the body, alleviating symptoms of dehydration such as fatigue, headaches, and dizziness. The best herbal capsules for dehydration include Astragalus membranaceus, Ginkgo biloba, Panax ginseng, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinchona officinalis, Rehmannia glutinosa, Zingiber officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Withania somnifera, and Panax quinquefolius. If left untreated, dehydration can lead to serious complications such as organ failure, seizures, and even death, severely impacting daily life and productivity, and affecting one's overall health and well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dehydration that are treated with herbal capsules are digestive issues like constipation, irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and diarrhea. These herbal supplements contain ingredients such as aloe vera, slippery elm, and marshmallow root that help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and promoting regular bowel movements.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with dehydration is a blend of Dandelion root, Licorice root, and Peppermint. Dandelion root aids in replenishing electrolytes and promoting fluid balance, while Licorice root helps to soothe and protect the digestive system. Peppermint, with its cooling properties, can help to stimulate digestion and increase water absorption. Together, these herbs work synergistically to replenish fluids, restore electrolyte balance, and promote overall hydration, providing relief from dehydration symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus capsules helps with dehydration because they stimulate the body's natural response to rehydrate. The herb's adaptogenic properties help regulate fluid balance, allowing the body to recover from dehydration more efficiently. Astragalus also contains antioxidants that help protect cells from damage caused by dehydration, promoting overall well-being. Additionally, it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate dehydration-related discomfort, such as headaches and fatigue, making it a popular natural remedy for dehydration relief. This helps the body to recover and replenish its fluid levels.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help combat dehydration by exerting antioxidant effects and enhancing cell membrane stability to maintain proper fluid balance.
Saponins: These compounds aid in dehydration relief by regulating electrolyte balance, improving blood circulation, and supporting overall kidney function to manage fluid balance.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides from Astragalus membranaceus help alleviate dehydration by stimulating the immune system, improving gut health, and facilitating the absorption of water and essential nutrients.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Astragalus membranaceus are commonly used for making capsules due to their high content of bioactive compounds.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Astragalus membranaceus are often used because they contain a concentrated amount of saponins and other medicinal properties.
Stems: The stems of Astragalus membranaceus are used to make capsules as they are rich in flavonoids and other beneficial compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of astragalus membranaceus roots in early morning for freshness and potency.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours to preserve properties.
Crush 20g of dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for easy encapsulation.
Mix 1g of the powder with 3g of vegetable cellulose and 1g of silicon dioxide to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the herbal powder blend and seal for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with dehydration because they improve blood flow to the kidneys, which enhances their ability to filter and produce urine. This process helps to flush out toxins and excess fluids from the body. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help to protect the kidneys from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dehydration. By supporting kidney function, Ginkgo biloba capsules can aid in the prevention and relief of dehydration. This makes it a potentially valuable supplement for individuals struggling with dehydration.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which can aid in preventing dehydration by ensuring proper circulation and reducing the risk of dehydration-related complications.

Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide and Ginkgolides): These terpenoids have antioxidant properties that help protect cells from damage and promote overall health, which can contribute to preventing dehydration by reducing oxidative stress and promoting cellular balance.

Triterpenoids (specifically Ginkgolides and Bilobalide): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and promote cellular health, which can aid in preventing dehydration by reducing inflammation and promoting proper fluid balance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which provide the medicinal properties associated with Ginkgo biloba.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used for their high content of flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, which are believed to have antioxidant and neuroprotective effects.
Barks: Ginkgo bark is sometimes used due to its potential to contain flavonoids and terpenoids, although it's less commonly used compared to leaves and seeds.
remedy_recipe: Measure 500 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and stems to prepare the herbal material.
Grind the ginkgo biloba material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix 10 grams of the ginkgo biloba powder with 10 grams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Encapsulate the mixture using a capsule machine set to a size of 0 number.
Label and store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng capsules helps with dehydration because they promote water retention in the body by stimulating the production of antidiuretic hormones. This hormone helps the kidneys to reabsorb water from the urine, thereby maintaining the body's water balance. Additionally, Panax ginseng capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the body caused by dehydration. By improving circulation and reducing oxidative stress, they also aid in the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to cells, making them an effective remedy for dehydration.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Help with dehydration by increasing water absorption in the body, improving electrolyte balance, and reducing oxidative stress.
Polyphenols: Help with dehydration by acting as antioxidants, reducing inflammation, and improving kidney function to promote efficient water and electrolyte management.
Saponins: Help with dehydration by enhancing the permeability of cell membranes, allowing for better absorption of water and electrolytes, and improving overall cardiovascular function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The primary component used due to its high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to provide medicinal benefits.
Rhyzomes: Sometimes used in addition to roots, as they also contain ginsenosides and other bioactive compounds.
Leaves: Although less commonly used than roots, the leaves of Panax ginseng are sometimes used to make capsules due to their potential antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of dried panax ginseng root material with 10% moisture content.
Grind the ginseng root material into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 2 grams of the ginseng powder with 8 grams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Use a capsule filler to fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powder mixture.
Store the herbal panax ginseng capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with dehydration because they contain licorice root extract, which has natural electrolyte-balancing properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to enhance the absorption of water in the body, helping to replenish fluids and electrolytes lost due to dehydration. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing dehydration caused by excessive sweating, diarrhea, or other factors. By promoting hydration and electrolyte balance, Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules can help alleviate symptoms and support overall health.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: This flavonoid compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce fluid loss and alleviate dehydration symptoms.
Licorice saponins: These triterpenoid saponins help to regulate fluid balance in the body by reducing water loss in the kidneys and improving the body's ability to retain fluids.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to dehydration symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain glycyrrhizin and other bioactive compounds, making them a popular choice for herbal supplements.
remedy_recipe: Collect 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or harvest them responsibly.
Crush the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the powder with 500 grams of gelatin and 100 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill 500 empty capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler for 20 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container away from direct sunlight and moisture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with dehydration because they contain quinine, a natural compound that acts as a diuretic to promote water loss in the body. However, it also stimulates the kidneys to produce more urine, which helps to flush out excess fluids and electrolytes, effectively replenishing the body's water balance. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis has been traditionally used to treat malaria, which often leads to dehydration, making it a valuable remedy for rehydrating the body.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps with dehydration by acting as a diuretic, which increases urine production to flush out excess fluids from the body.
Quinovic acid glycosides: These compounds help with dehydration by acting as diuretics and also by reducing inflammation in the body, which can be a contributing factor to dehydration.
Quinovic acid: Quinovic acid helps with dehydration by acting as an anti-inflammatory and also by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can lead to dehydration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Cinchona officinalis due to their high content of quinine, a key ingredient in treating dehydration and malaria.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis trees are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, including quinine, which helps to prevent and treat dehydration and other conditions.
Stems: Some parts of Cinchona officinalis use stems as an alternative to leaves and barks, but it is less common and not as widely recognized for its medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Crush 20g of dried cinchona officinalis bark into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 50g of gelatin and 20g of vegetable glycerin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 10ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir well to dissolve the gelatin for 2 minutes.
Use a capsule filler to fill 100 empty capsules with the mixture taking 15 minutes per capsule.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rehmannia glutinosa capsules helps with dehydration because they support the body's natural ability to retain fluids. The herb is believed to nourish and protect the kidneys, which play a crucial role in regulating fluid balance. By maintaining healthy kidney function, Rehmannia glutinosa capsules may help prevent excessive fluid loss and promote overall hydration. Additionally, the herb's diuretic properties help regulate urine production, ensuring that the body retains the right amount of fluids. This balance can aid in replenishing fluids and electrolytes.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help with dehydration by increasing water and electrolyte absorption in the body, thus replenishing lost fluids.

Steroids: Steroids in Rehmannia glutinosa capsules help with dehydration by reducing inflammation and improving kidney function, which in turn aids in the efficient removal of excess fluids from the body.
Saponins: Saponins have a diuretic effect, which helps increase urine production and remove excess fluids from the body, thus helping to alleviate dehydration symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of active compounds and medicinal properties.
Rhyzomes: They are also used, as they contain similar compounds to the roots, which contribute to their medicinal value.
Seeds: They are occasionally used, as they contain some medicinal properties, although to a lesser extent than roots and rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15g of rehmannia glutinosa root in spring or autumn for best quality and potency.
Dry the rehmannia glutinosa root in a warm place for 2-3 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind 5g of dried rehmannia glutinosa root into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground rehmannia glutinosa powder with 1-2g of gum arabic as a natural binder.
Fill the powder mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with dehydration because of its natural diuretic properties, which help to stimulate urine production and flush out excess fluids from the body. The active compound, gingerol, has anti-inflammatory effects that aid in reducing the severity of dehydration symptoms, such as nausea and headaches. Additionally, Zingiber officinale capsules can help to replenish lost electrolytes, including potassium and sodium, which are essential for maintaining proper hydration levels in the body. This makes it an effective remedy for dehydration.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help with dehydration by reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which can help alleviate symptoms of dehydration such as headaches and fatigue.
Shogaols: Assist in dehydration relief by acting as a natural diuretic, helping to increase urine production and remove excess fluids and electrolytes from the body.
Volatile oils (including Zingiberene): Help to alleviate dehydration symptoms by reducing nausea and improving digestion, which can help the body absorb essential fluids and electrolytes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain the active compounds with medicinal properties, such as gingerols and shogaols, responsible for its anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits.
Roots: They are the primary source of ginger's bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
Barks (Note: Not typically used, however it is sometimes used): The bark of the rhizome can be used, but it is less commonly used than the rhizome and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh zingiber officinale roots with at least 4% curcumin content for optimal results.
Dry the harvested zingiber officinale roots in a single layer at 50c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 2g of the powdered zingiber officinale with 2g of filler material like potato starch in a small bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the zingiber officinale powder mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with dehydration because they promote overall immune function and hydration. This herb contains compounds that stimulate the production of cytokines, which help the body to fight off infections and inflammation caused by dehydration. By supporting the immune system, Echinacea purpurea capsules can help the body to recover from dehydration more quickly. Additionally, some herbalists believe that Echinacea purpurea has a diuretic effect, which can help to flush out excess fluids and toxins, further aiding in the recovery from dehydration.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with dehydration by acting as antioxidants, which reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, thereby helping to maintain fluid balance.
Caffeic acid: It helps with dehydration by acting as a diuretic, which helps to increase urine production and promote fluid balance in the body.

Iridoids: They help with dehydration by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, thereby helping to maintain fluid balance and prevent dehydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, making them a popular choice for supplements.
Leaves: They are easier to harvest and process than roots, making them a more accessible and cost-effective option.
Flowers: They have a high concentration of alkaloids, which are believed to have medicinal properties, making them a popular choice for supplements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea roots and flowers using a dehydrator or air drying method for 24 hours.
Grind the dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the ground echinacea purpurea powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or vegetable cellulose to form a uniform blend.
Fill the mixture into empty capsules using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera capsules helps with dehydration because they promote the body's natural ability to retain water and electrolytes. The adaptogenic properties of Withania somnifera help to regulate the body's stress response, which can contribute to dehydration. Additionally, the herb has been shown to improve kidney function and increase urine production, which helps to maintain proper fluid balance. By supporting the body's natural hydration processes, Withania somnifera capsules can help alleviate dehydration symptoms and promote overall health and well-being. This can be especially beneficial during times of physical exertion or environmental stress.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect the body from oxidative stress caused by dehydration.
Alkaloids (Withanine and Withananine): These compounds have diuretic properties, which help to increase urine production and promote the removal of excess fluids from the body.

Saponins (Withanosides): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to maintain the integrity of the epithelial barrier and prevent water loss through the skin, thereby reducing dehydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of bioactive compounds, such as withanolides, which provide the medicinal properties of the plant.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and contain a significant amount of withanolides, making them a valuable addition to dehydrated capsules.
Seeds: They contain a high concentration of withanolides and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry the withania somnifera roots for 2 hours in a low-temperature oven at 150°f.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 200mg of the powder with 20mg of gelatin and 10mg of glycerin.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately with a capsule sealer.
Store the withania somnifera capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax quinquefolius capsules helps with dehydration because they contain adaptogenic compounds that aid in regulating the body's fluid balance. The ginsenosides present in these capsules are known to stimulate the kidneys, increasing urine production and promoting the elimination of excess fluids. Additionally, Panax quinquefolius capsules may help reduce inflammation and promote cellular hydration, which can contribute to improved water retention and overall hydration levels. This may be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing dehydration due to excessive sweating, heat, or other environmental factors.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with dehydration by enhancing fluid and electrolyte balance in the body, contributing to the relief of dehydration symptoms.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids, particularly ginsenosides, may aid in hydration by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to dehydration.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including quercetin, may help alleviate dehydration symptoms by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, thus supporting hydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax quinquefolius are commonly used due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have medicinal properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they are rich in vitamins and minerals, and are often dried and processed into capsules for their perceived health benefits.
Barks: The barks of Panax quinquefolius are used due to their high content of ginsenosides and other bioactive compounds, which are believed to promote overall health and well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest panax quinquefolius roots in late summer to early fall when they are fully mature.
Dry the roots in a warm dry place at 30 to 40 degrees celsius for two to three weeks.
Grind 30 to 60 grams of dried panax quinquefolius roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 30 to 60 grams of gelatin or vegetable capsules in a small bowl.
Fill 100 to 200 vegetable or gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal immediately.
